html_content:
INSIGHT: The Single Most Important Thing
Ola spent ₹507.7 Cr on R&D in FY23 (18.3% of revenue) across India, UK, and US — capitalising 83% as intangible assets — but with only 164K units sold in FY26, the amortisation burden per vehicle is structurally high for a low-volume manufacturer.

R&D Investment

---

R&D Spend

| Fiscal Year | R&D Spend (₹ Cr) | % of Total Income |
|-------------|-----------------|-------------------|
| FY21 | 43.6 | 41.1% |
| FY22 | 175.8 | 38.5% |
| FY23 | 507.7 | 18.3% |
| Q1 FY24 (annualized) | ~374 | 7.3% |

---

R&D Breakdown (FY23)

| Component | Amount (₹ Cr) |
|-----------|---------------|
| Research costs (expensed) | 86.1 |
| Additions to intangible assets under development | 421.6 |
| Total R&D Spend | 507.7 |

---

R&D Locations

  • India — Core vehicle design, manufacturing process
  • UK — Advanced engineering
  • US — Software, AI, connected features

---

What's Being Developed

In-House Designed Components

  • Electric powertrain (motor + drivetrain)
  • Battery pack + BMS (Battery Management System)
  • Power electronics modules
  • Vehicle frames
  • MoveOS software

Under Development

  • 4680 form factor cells (BIC)
  • Cell manufacturing processes (Gigafactory)
  • Motorcycle platform (4 models)
  • Gen 3+ platform evolution

---

Capitalized Development

Significant R&D is capitalized as "intangible assets under development":


  • FY22: ₹160.2 Cr additions

  • FY23: ₹421.6 Cr additions

  • Q1 FY24: ₹82.7 Cr additions

This means a large portion of R&D spend doesn't hit the P&L immediately but creates future amortization burden.

---

Data Sources

DATA: Source: PROFIT_AND_LOSS.csv
R&D spend was ₹507.7 Cr in FY23 vs ₹175.8 Cr in FY22, with ₹421.6 Cr (83%) capitalised as intangible assets under development — shifting current expense to future amortisation burden.
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv
R&D investment in the Gigafactory (4680/46100 cells) delivered BOM reduction from 0% to 30%, translating to gross margin improvement from 12% (Q4 FY25) to 36% (Q4 FY26) — a 24pp expansion.
DATA: Source: EV_SEGMENT_COMPARISON.csv
Ola's revenue/unit at ₹223,902 is the highest in the industry partly because each unit carries disproportionate R&D amortisation from a small sales base (TVS 371K units vs Ola 164K).
DATA: Source: QUARTERLY_FINANCIALS_EXT.csv
EBITDA went from -22.1% (Q4 FY25) to +4.5% (Q4 FY26) — proving the R&D investments in Gen 2 platform and Gigafactory cells were finally bearing fruit after 3 years of spending.
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv
Future R&D targets: LFP 46100 cells at $80/kWh aiming for $70/kWh by Mar 2028, and Gen 3+ platform — requiring continued high investment despite cash constraints and ₹3,516 Cr cash (Mar 25).
DATA: Source: market/PRODUCT_SPECS_COMPARISON.csv
Ather Rizta at Rs 109,000 defines a new 'Family EV Scooter' category with 56L boot space (largest in EV scooters), 2.9kWh battery, and 123 km range. No competitor directly matches this family-focused positioning.
Causal chain: Ather identified that families need storage space (56L boot fits two helmets) more than speed, creating a differentiated position that competitors (focused on performance) have not addressed
Cells: Row 15 (Rizta): Price=109000, Battery=2.9kWh, Range=123km, Boot_Space=56L
DATA: Source: PRODUCT_MIX_QUARTERLY.csv
Cell source evolution: Imported (Q1-Q3 FY25) → Mixed Import+NMC pilot (Q4 FY25) → In-house NMC 4680 (Q1-Q3 FY26) → Mixed NMC+LFP 46100 pilot (Q4 FY26). This mirrors the Gigafactory production ramp.
Causal chain: The shift from imported cells to in-house NMC was always the plan; the addition of LFP 46100 was a strategic pivot announced in Apr 2026.
Cells: Rows 2-9: Cell_Source
DATA: Source: financial_mda.txt
R&D spend in FY23: Rs 5,077 Mn (18.25% of total income). FY22: Rs 1,758 Mn (38.54%). Three-months ended Jun-2023: Rs 936 Mn (7.32%). R&D as % of income declined as revenue grew faster than R&D investment.
Causal chain: R&D spending grew in absolute terms but declined as % of revenue, suggesting R&D scale benefits were leveraged across growing sales.
Cells: Pages 315-316: R&D spend details
DATA: Source: financial_mda.txt
68.85% of components were common across S1 Pro, S1 Air, and S1 X+ models as of Oct 2023, demonstrating the platform approach. Gen 2 platform started manufacturing Jul 2023 with enhanced specs (acceleration, speed, range, motor power, battery).
Causal chain: High component commonality across models reduced per-unit costs and sped up new model development, enabling the rapid S1 X launch.
Cells: Page 318: Platform commonality and Gen 2 details
DATA: Source: Ola_after_ipo.txt
LFP chemistry advantages: 30-40% cheaper raw materials (no cobalt/nickel), better thermal stability for 45C+ Indian summers, 3,000+ cycles vs 1,500 for NMC. Trade-off: ~15% lower energy density (acceptable for 100-150km scooter range).
Causal chain: The LFP pivot addresses both cost (30-40% cheaper raw materials) and Indian climate suitability (thermal stability), making it the right chemistry for the Indian mass market.
Cells: Section 4: LFP pivot rationale
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP R&D spend details: FY21 Rs 43.61 Cr (Rs 436.06M - 41.11% of total income), FY22 Rs 175.84 Cr (Rs 1,758.40M - 38.54%), FY23 Rs 507.71 Cr (Rs 5,077.11M - 18.25%), Q1 FY24 Rs 93.55 Cr (Rs 935.53M - 7.32%). R&D as % of income declined as revenue grew faster than R&D investment.
Context: Pages 315, 320
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP R&D spend composition: Research costs (expensed): FY21 Rs 6.99 Cr, FY22 Rs 15.69 Cr, FY23 Rs 86.08 Cr, Q1 FY24 Rs 10.82 Cr. Additions to intangible assets under development (capitalized): FY21 Rs 36.62 Cr, FY22 Rs 160.15 Cr, FY23 Rs 421.63 Cr, Q1 FY24 Rs 82.74 Cr. Majority of R&D spend was capitalized as intangible assets under development.
Context: Page 315
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP R&D locations and focus: Activities in India, UK and US focused on designing and developing core EV components and new EV products. Battery Innovation Centre (BIC) focused on developing cell and battery technology and manufacturing processes. R&D centered on motor and drivetrain, battery packs, electronics, software, and MoveOS.
Context: Pages 315, 317-318
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP development expenditure capitalization criteria: Costs capitalized only if expenditure can be measured reliably, product/process is technically and commercially feasible, future economic benefits are probable, and the company intends to and has sufficient resources to complete development. Capitalized costs include materials, R&D employee costs, and directly attributable overhead.
Context: Page 315
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP intangible assets: Product development (internally generated) amortized over 5 years; Computer software over 3 years; Domain name over 10 years; Goodwill and "Ola" brand (trade name) have indefinite life. Right to use "Ola" trade name has indefinite useful life, reviewed annually.
Context: Page 325
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP CapEx on development expenditure on internally generated intangible assets: FY22 Rs 110.33 Cr (Rs 1,103.30M), FY23 Rs 403.03 Cr (Rs 4,030.31M), Q1 FY24 Rs 75.33 Cr (Rs 753.34M). These capitalized development costs represent investments in platform technology, MoveOS, and core EV components.
Context: Page 321
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/financial_mda.txt
DRHP stated that R&D spend is expected to increase for the foreseeable future as the company continues to invest in expanding product offerings and cost-efficient manufacturing. The Gen 2 platform (July 2023) with 68.85% component commonality was a direct output of this R&D investment.
Context: Pages 315, 318
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Patent portfolio at DRHP date: 35 registered patents in India, 205 patent applications pending in India. 10 registered patents in US, UK, China, Netherlands. 37 patent applications pending in US, Japan, Great Britain, Australia, PCT, and European Patent Office. No assurance that pending applications will result in issued patents.
Context: Page 53, Risk Factor 36
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Ola does NOT own the 'Ola' trademark — it is licensed from ANI Technologies (Ola Cabs). Indian IP protection has "historically been deficient and ineffective." Implementation and enforcement of IP laws in India "may not be as effective as in the United States or other developed countries."
Context: Page 53, Risk Factor 36
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Technology cost breakdown: ₹94.31M (FY21), ₹373.44M (FY22), ₹988.30M (FY23), ₹310.67M (Q1 FY24). As % of revenue: 1,091.55% (FY21 — pre-revenue), 10.00% (FY22), 3.76% (FY23), 2.50% (Q1 FY24).
Context: Page 41, Risk Factor 14
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Ola's R&D team: 974 on-roll and off-roll employees dedicated to R&D as of October 31, 2023, including 60 PhD holders. R&D across five key technologies: software (MoveOS), electronics, motor and drivetrain, cells and battery packs, and manufacturing technology.
Context: Page 162 & 164, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Ola has developed the preliminary design of a 4680 cell in-house at BIC. BIC equipment includes material synthesis, cell manufacturing technology, material characterization, prototyping, and testing. Currently focused on NMC formulations but evaluating other chemistries including LFP.
Context: Page 181, Our Business - Cell Technology
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/risk_factors.txt
Pre-IPO risk: Ola may not be able to "innovate or innovate at the speed of some of our competitors." Technology or platform "may become obsolete" resulting in "loss of market share." New technologies may be used "ineffectively" or Ola may "fail to adapt to emerging industry standards or regulatory requirements."
Context: Page 37-38, Risk Factor 7

Connections

Causes: Technology/4680 Cell Tech, Technology/Gen 2 Platform

Effects: Strategy/Backward Integration, Strategy/Product_Mix_Cannibalization

Amplifiers: Technology/Gigafactory_Updates

Linked Nodes: Technology/MoveOS, Strategy/EV Hub Tamil Nadu
